{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Brand Communications Manager",
  "description": "Darey.io is a&nbsp;DevOps virtual learning Bootcamp.&nbsp;In the Darey.io social learning program, you will take your knowledge and certifications and apply them to solve real problems. You may start with little or no IT knowledge but, after completing 30 hands-on projects, you are as good as other engineers with 3 years in the industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Brand Communications Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Job Type: Full Time

Role Summary


	This is not a content creation role.
	This is a&nbsp;content operations and strategy role&nbsp;responsible for running a high-volume, multi-brand content engine across the Darey.io ecosystem.
	You will own the&nbsp;end-to-end content machine&nbsp;from strategy to production, distribution, and performance ensuring that all brands show up consistently, clearly, and at scale.
	You are not here to &ldquo;post content.&rdquo;
	You are here to&nbsp;build, run, and optimize a system that drives growth.


About the Ecosystem:


	You will be responsible for content across three interconnected brands:


Darey.io (Tech Education):


	Focus: Tech career transformation
	Goal: Drive learner acquisition
	Content Angle: Transformation stories, career pathways, job outcomes


Xterns.ai (Work Experience Platform):


	Focus: Talent placement &amp; work experience
	Goal: Attract talent + build employer trust
	Content Angle: Proof of work, real-world experience, hiring success


Dare (Founder Brand):


	Focus: Thought leadership &amp; trust building
	Goal: Build credibility across the ecosystem
	Content Angle: Insights, storytelling, tech career guidance


Key Responsibilities
Content Strategy &amp; Brand Positioning:


	Own and evolve the content strategy across all brands
	Develop&nbsp;monthly content calendars&nbsp;aligned with campaigns and growth goals
	Maintain&nbsp;consistent brand voice&nbsp;across platforms
	Write or oversee founder-led content (especially LinkedIn)
	Translate business goals into&nbsp;clear content direction


Production &amp; Content Operations:


	Plan and execute&nbsp;monthly batch shoot days (3 per month)
	Create shoot plans, scripts, and content briefs
	Coordinate videographers and production logistics
	Oversee post-production workflow (editing, feedback, approvals)
	Ensure&nbsp;minimum output of 80 videos monthly


Distribution &amp; Platform Management:
Manage publishing across:


	LinkedIn
	YouTube
	Instagram
	TikTok
	Schedule content using&nbsp;GoHighLevel (GHL)
	Set up and manage&nbsp;content automations and funnels
	Ensure consistency in posting cadence (no gaps)


Content Repurposing System:


	Turn long-form content into multiple short-form assets:
	Shorts/Reels/TikToks
	Carousels
	Quote graphics
	Written posts
	Build and manage a&nbsp;repeatable repurposing pipeline
	Maximize content value across platforms


Team Leadership &amp; Coordination:


	Manage and supervise:
	Video Editors
	Videographers
	Graphic Designer
	Assign tasks, track delivery, and maintain quality standards
	Ensure editors meet weekly output targets
	Provide structured feedback and performance guidance


Performance Tracking &amp; Optimization:


	Track and analyze:
	Views
	Engagement rates
	Follower growth
	Platform-specific KPIs
	Produce&nbsp;monthly performance reports
	Identify winning content and scale it
	Fix underperforming areas with clear action plans


The Content Machine You Will Run:


	80 videos per month
	3 batch shoot days monthly
	Multi-platform distribution system
	Structured repurposing pipeline
	Automated scheduling via GHL
	This system already exists.
	Your job is to run it, improve it, and never let it break.


Requirements
Experience:


	3&ndash;5 years in:
	Content strategy
	Brand communications
	Content production or media operations
	Experience managing&nbsp;multi-platform content at scale
	Strong exposure to&nbsp;video production workflows
	Familiarity with African/Nigerian digital audience


Core Skills:


	Strong writing and storytelling ability
	Content planning and execution
	Project management (high-volume workflows)
	Deep understanding of social platforms &amp; algorithms
	Data-driven decision-making
	Team management and coordination.
",

Brand Communications Manager Job at Darey.io




        
    

        
        Darey.io is a DevOps virtual learning Bootcamp. In the Darey.io social learning program, you will take your knowledge and certifications and apply them to solve real problems. You may start with little or no IT knowledge but, after completing 30 hands-on projects, you are as good as other engineers with 3 years in the industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Brand Communications Manager

Job Location: Lagos
Job Type: Full Time

Role Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	This is not a content creation role.
	This is a content operations and strategy role responsible for running a high-volume, multi-brand content engine across the Darey.io ecosystem.
	You will own the end-to-end content machine from strategy to production, distribution, and performance ensuring that all brands show up consistently, clearly, and at scale.
	You are not here to “post content.”
	You are here to build, run, and optimize a system that drives growth.


About the Ecosystem:


	You will be responsible for content across three interconnected brands:


Darey.io (Tech Education):


	Focus: Tech career transformation
	Goal: Drive learner acquisition
	Content Angle: Transformation stories, career pathways, job outcomes


Xterns.ai (Work Experience Platform):


	Focus: Talent placement & work experience
	Goal: Attract talent + build employer trust
	Content Angle: Proof of work, real-world experience, hiring success


Dare (Founder Brand):


	Focus: Thought leadership & trust building
	Goal: Build credibility across the ecosystem
	Content Angle: Insights, storytelling, tech career guidance


Key Responsibilities
Content Strategy & Brand Positioning:


	Own and evolve the content strategy across all brands
	Develop monthly content calendars aligned with campaigns and growth goals
	Maintain consistent brand voice across platforms
	Write or oversee founder-led content (especially LinkedIn)
	Translate business goals into clear content direction


Production & Content Operations:


	Plan and execute monthly batch shoot days (3 per month)
	Create shoot plans, scripts, and content briefs
	Coordinate videographers and production logistics
	Oversee post-production workflow (editing, feedback, approvals)
	Ensure minimum output of 80 videos monthly


Distribution & Platform Management:
Manage publishing across:


	LinkedIn
	YouTube
	Instagram
	TikTok
	Schedule content using GoHighLevel (GHL)
	Set up and manage content automations and funnels
	Ensure consistency in posting cadence (no gaps)


Content Repurposing System:


	Turn long-form content into multiple short-form assets:
	Shorts/Reels/TikToks
	Carousels
	Quote graphics
	Written posts
	Build and manage a repeatable repurposing pipeline
	Maximize content value across platforms


Team Leadership & Coordination:


	Manage and supervise:
	Video Editors
	Videographers
	Graphic Designer
	Assign tasks, track delivery, and maintain quality standards
	Ensure editors meet weekly output targets
	Provide structured feedback and performance guidance


Performance Tracking & Optimization:


	Track and analyze:
	Views
	Engagement rates
	Follower growth
	Platform-specific KPIs
	Produce monthly performance reports
	Identify winning content and scale it
	Fix underperforming areas with clear action plans


The Content Machine You Will Run:


	80 videos per month
	3 batch shoot days monthly
	Multi-platform distribution system
	Structured repurposing pipeline
	Automated scheduling via GHL
	This system already exists.
	Your job is to run it, improve it, and never let it break.


Requirements
Experience:


	3–5 years in:
	Content strategy
	Brand communications
	Content production or media operations
	Experience managing multi-platform content at scale
	Strong exposure to video production workflows
	Familiarity with African/Nigerian digital audience


Core Skills:


	Strong writing and storytelling ability
	Content planning and execution
	Project management (high-volume workflows)
	Deep understanding of social platforms & algorithms
	Data-driven decision-making
	Team management and coordination.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "title": "Internal Control Officer",
  "description": "Bosak Microfinance Bank Limited was incorporated in December 2009 with the business objective of providing financial service to micro clients who were excluded from the mainstream financial system. It commenced operations in June 2010 upon obtaining a Unit Microfinance Banking License from the Central Bank of Nigeria. It later obtained a State Microfinance Banking License in 2016. Bosak Microfinance Bank Limited&rsquo;s financial inclusion effort focuses on promoting savings culture, access to credit facilities for income-generation purposes and reducing vulnerability to risk through micro insurance.

The Bank&rsquo;s major strategic focus employs the group lending methodology in its operations with a leaning towards women. It believes in the commitment of women folk to work hard and help their families conquer poverty. As such, more than 95% of the Bank&rsquo;s lending activities focuses on women. The success of the bank&rsquo;s group lending activities largely depends on group solidarity and cohesion. The success recorded by the bank in its lending program has earned it strategic partnership with the Bank of Industry (BOI) and the Development Bank of Nigeria (DBN) among others. The combination of cutting-edge technology, a strong and committed Board of Directors and motivated employees has helped the Bank to create a brand widely accepted by its teeming clients in Lagos. It is now expanding its frontiers in a scaling program aimed at delivering improved value to its stakeholders.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Internal Control Officer
Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Assess key risks facing the bank and identify appropriate mitigation measures using the Risk Management Graduation Model (RMGM).
	Develop, analyze, and disseminate risk measurements, reports, and dashboards to support management decision-making.
	Manage enterprise-wide risks across all units of the bank and follow up to ensure timely compliance with risk observations and recommendations.
	Handle customer complaints received via the Toll-Free Telephone Line, investigate reported issues, and escalate findings to the Unit Head for management action.
	Review and verify Loan Officers&rsquo; daily transactions to ensure accuracy and compliance with internal policies.
	Perform quality control checks on transactions and operational processes.
	Conduct call-over of transactions to confirm correctness and completeness.
	Carry out any other ad hoc duties as may be assigned by management.


Why Work with Bosak Microfinance Bank Limited?


	If you desire to work in an organization where employee motivation is key and passion is rewarded, then Bosak MFB might be the place for you.
	We are committed to building an open and rewarding work environment where all employees have equal opportunities for growth to realize their full potential.
	Our remuneration and employee welfare packages are among the best in the industry.


We offer the following Benefits:


	Salary: N300,000-400,000 Monthly.
	HMO
	Constant Training and Development
	Leave &amp; Passage Allowance


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;recruitment@bosakmfb.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Internal Control Officer Job at Bosak Microfinance Bank




        
    

        
        Bosak Microfinance Bank Limited was incorporated in December 2009 with the business objective of providing financial service to micro clients who were excluded from the mainstream financial system. It commenced operations in June 2010 upon obtaining a Unit Microfinance Banking License from the Central Bank of Nigeria. It later obtained a State Microfinance Banking License in 2016. Bosak Microfinance Bank Limited’s financial inclusion effort focuses on promoting savings culture, access to credit facilities for income-generation purposes and reducing vulnerability to risk through micro insurance.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



The Bank’s major strategic focus employs the group lending methodology in its operations with a leaning towards women. It believes in the commitment of women folk to work hard and help their families conquer poverty. As such, more than 95% of the Bank’s lending activities focuses on women. The success of the bank’s group lending activities largely depends on group solidarity and cohesion. The success recorded by the bank in its lending program has earned it strategic partnership with the Bank of Industry (BOI) and the Development Bank of Nigeria (DBN) among others. The combination of cutting-edge technology, a strong and committed Board of Directors and motivated employees has helped the Bank to create a brand widely accepted by its teeming clients in Lagos. It is now expanding its frontiers in a scaling program aimed at delivering improved value to its stakeholders.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Internal Control Officer
Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Assess key risks facing the bank and identify appropriate mitigation measures using the Risk Management Graduation Model (RMGM).
	Develop, analyze, and disseminate risk measurements, reports, and dashboards to support management decision-making.
	Manage enterprise-wide risks across all units of the bank and follow up to ensure timely compliance with risk observations and recommendations.
	Handle customer complaints received via the Toll-Free Telephone Line, investigate reported issues, and escalate findings to the Unit Head for management action.
	Review and verify Loan Officers’ daily transactions to ensure accuracy and compliance with internal policies.
	Perform quality control checks on transactions and operational processes.
	Conduct call-over of transactions to confirm correctness and completeness.
	Carry out any other ad hoc duties as may be assigned by management.


Why Work with Bosak Microfinance Bank Limited?


	If you desire to work in an organization where employee motivation is key and passion is rewarded, then Bosak MFB might be the place for you.
	We are committed to building an open and rewarding work environment where all employees have equal opportunities for growth to realize their full potential.
	Our remuneration and employee welfare packages are among the best in the industry.


We offer the following Benefits:


	Salary: N300,000-400,000 Monthly.
	HMO
	Constant Training and Development
	Leave & Passage Allowance


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "title": "Operation Manager",
  "description": "Mshel Homes Limited is a real estate development and marketing company incorporated in 2018 with a unique vision to be the leading real estate company in Nigeria, with an outstanding reputation for honesty, integrity and impeccable customer service.

At Mshel Homes Limited our mission is to create a lifetime relationship with our clientele by deliverying property solutions through innovative and technological driven processes that caters to your real estate needs whilst adhering to the highest standards, systems and performance goals necessary to achieving your dreams.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Operation Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja

Responsibilties


	Overseeing Daily Operations: Managing everyday business activities to ensure smooth and efficient workflow&nbsp;
	Manage budgets, control costs, and optimize resource allocation: Planning and managing expenses to stay within financial limits&nbsp;
	Facilities Management: Oversee office infrastructure, maintenance, utilities, and workplace safety to ensure a productive work environment.&nbsp;
	Resource &amp; Asset Management: Manage company assets, supplies, and inventory to ensure proper utilization and cost control.&nbsp;
	Vendor &amp; Contract Management: Manage relationships with suppliers, service providers, and contractors to ensure quality service delivery.&nbsp;
	Compliance &amp; Governance: Ensure administrative operations comply with company policies, legal requirements, and regulatory standards.&nbsp;
	Records &amp; Documentation Management: Maintain accurate records, files, and documentation systems for easy access and compliance.&nbsp;
	Risk &amp; Security Management: Oversee office security, safety protocols, and risk mitigation measures.&nbsp;
	Cross-Department Coordination: Support and collaborate with other departments to meet organizational needs and objectives.&nbsp;


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business Administration, Operations Management, or related field
	4&ndash;8 years of relevant experience in operations or management
	Strong leadership and team management skills
	Excellent organizational and problem-solving abilities
	Good knowledge of operational best practices and compliance standards
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and operational software tools.


Salary
N350,000 - N500,000 monthly.",

Operation Manager Job at Mshel Homes Limited




        
    

        
        Mshel Homes Limited is a real estate development and marketing company incorporated in 2018 with a unique vision to be the leading real estate company in Nigeria, with an outstanding reputation for honesty, integrity and impeccable customer service.

At Mshel Homes Limited our mission is to create a lifetime relationship with our clientele by deliverying property solutions through innovative and technological driven processes that caters to your real estate needs whilst adhering to the highest standards, systems and performance goals necessary to achieving your dreams.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operation Manager

Job Location: Abuja
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Responsibilties


	Overseeing Daily Operations: Managing everyday business activities to ensure smooth and efficient workflow 
	Manage budgets, control costs, and optimize resource allocation: Planning and managing expenses to stay within financial limits 
	Facilities Management: Oversee office infrastructure, maintenance, utilities, and workplace safety to ensure a productive work environment. 
	Resource & Asset Management: Manage company assets, supplies, and inventory to ensure proper utilization and cost control. 
	Vendor & Contract Management: Manage relationships with suppliers, service providers, and contractors to ensure quality service delivery. 
	Compliance & Governance: Ensure administrative operations comply with company policies, legal requirements, and regulatory standards. 
	Records & Documentation Management: Maintain accurate records, files, and documentation systems for easy access and compliance. 
	Risk & Security Management: Oversee office security, safety protocols, and risk mitigation measures. 
	Cross-Department Coordination: Support and collaborate with other departments to meet organizational needs and objectives. 


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Operations Management, or related field
	4–8 years of relevant experience in operations or management
	Strong leadership and team management skills
	Excellent organizational and problem-solving abilities
	Good knowledge of operational best practices and compliance standards
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and operational software tools.


Salary
N350,000 - N500,000 monthly.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "title": "Personal Assistant",
  "description": "Star Edge Public Health Concept is a consultancy firm concerned withEnvironmental Sanitation, Pest &amp; Vector Control, System Development &amp; Management, Capacity Building &amp; System, Strengthening for food safety &amp; environmental Health Response, Environmental Hazard Prevention &amp; Control, Environmental impact assessment, environmental auditing, Restoration work, Renovation, Energy, Engineering Installations, Urban structure, Events Management, engineering and architecture, landscape infrastructure, hydraulics &amp; environment, telecoms trading, sales and distribution of general goods &amp; general contractors.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Personal Assistant

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time&nbsp;(Monday to Saturday)

Job Responsibilities


	Manage schedules, appointments, and meetings for the Executive Director
	Handle correspondence, emails, and official communications
	Prepare reports, documents, and presentations as required
	Coordinate travel arrangements and logistics
	Maintain confidentiality and handle sensitive information professionally
	Perform other administrative duties as assigned


Requirements


	Minimum of HND/BSc in any relevant field
	Proven experience as a Personal Assistant or in a similar role is an advantage
	Excellent organizational and time management skills
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)
	High level of discretion and professionalism.


Salary
N150,000 monthly.",

Personal Assistant Job at Star Edge Public Health Concept




        
    

        
        Star Edge Public Health Concept is a consultancy firm concerned withEnvironmental Sanitation, Pest & Vector Control, System Development & Management, Capacity Building & System, Strengthening for food safety & environmental Health Response, Environmental Hazard Prevention & Control, Environmental impact assessment, environmental auditing, Restoration work, Renovation, Energy, Engineering Installations, Urban structure, Events Management, engineering and architecture, landscape infrastructure, hydraulics & environment, telecoms trading, sales and distribution of general goods & general contractors.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Personal Assistant
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time (Monday to Saturday)

Job Responsibilities


	Manage schedules, appointments, and meetings for the Executive Director
	Handle correspondence, emails, and official communications
	Prepare reports, documents, and presentations as required
	Coordinate travel arrangements and logistics
	Maintain confidentiality and handle sensitive information professionally
	Perform other administrative duties as assigned


Requirements


	Minimum of HND/BSc in any relevant field
	Proven experience as a Personal Assistant or in a similar role is an advantage
	Excellent organizational and time management skills
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)
	High level of discretion and professionalism.


Salary
N150,000 monthly.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Head of Innovation & Operations Job at Leanx Support and Consulting




        
    

        
        Leanx Support and Consulting - We are a business development service provider, recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head of Innovation & Operations

Job Location: Abeokuta, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are recruiting a Head of Innovation & Operations with strong execution, leadership, and systems management experience.


Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Oversee operations across programs, partnerships, and administrative functions
	Design and implement systems, workflows, and performance frameworks (KPIs)
	Lead innovation initiatives and drive new products and revenue opportunities
	Manage programs and donor-funded projects, ensuring quality delivery and compliance
	Build and manage strategic partnerships and stakeholder relationships


Requirements


	4–6 years of experience in Operations, Strategy, Innovation, or related roles
	Strong leadership, execution, and organizational management skills
	Experience managing programs or donor-funded projects is an advantage.


Salary
N300,000 / month.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
